name: Operator Silas Wickes
publicFace: A steady, if slow, company man — his colleagues call him a plug behind his back but he has never made a consequential error.
privateGoal: Get through his shift without incident and collect his weekly wage.
fear: Being blamed for a transmission error that causes a federal problem — he has a family and no savings.
knowledge: He received the red fire dispatch and forwarded it per protocol.
He received what appeared to be a routine location update from the Sandusky station that contradicted the red fire route — he is not sure which to believe.
He knows the Sandusky night operator is fast — faster than him — and fast operators sometimes send things that look wrong but are not.
He is about to request a repeat of the contradictory location update, which will expose the phantom if Caleb cannot respond convincingly.
voice: Hesitant, apologetic, uses protocol language — always refers to procedure when uncertain.
trustLogic: Follows whoever sounds most authoritative and most certain. Can be manipulated by confident technical language.
secrets: He once lost a message for six hours because he fell asleep at the key — he covered it by claiming line interference. No one ever verified this.

aggressionProfile.mildPressure: Defers immediately and asks what the correct procedure should be.
aggressionProfile.heavyPressure: Retreats to the rulebook — says he is only doing what protocol requires.
aggressionProfile.breakingPoint: He will not admit the sleeping incident.
